title: Urban Waters Federal Partnership Act of 2025
short_title: Urban Waters Federal Partnership Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill creates a new program to help cities reconnect with their rivers, lakes, and other waterways. The program focuses on helping poor neighborhoods that have been cut off from their local waters. Different government agencies will work together to give money and support to projects that clean up the water, create parks and recreation areas, and help communities use their waterways again. The goal is to make urban waters cleaner and give people better access to enjoy them.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, and in addition to the Committees on Natural Resources, and Appropriations, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, i

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Urban Waters Federal Partnership Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Definitions
sections.text: In this law, these terms mean the following. Administrator means the person who leads the Environmental Protection Agency. Member agencies means all of these government departments and agencies: the Environmental Protection Agency, the Department of the Interior, the Department of Agriculture, the Corps of Engineers, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, the Economic Development Administration, the Department of Housing and Urban Development, the Department of Transportation, the Department of Energy, the Department of Education, the National Institute for Environmental Health Sciences, the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund, the Federal Emergency Management Agency, the Corporation for National and Community Service, and any other agencies that choose to join the Urban Waters program if their mission and funding allows them to participate. Secretaries means the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture. Urban Waters ambassador means a person who lives near an Urban Waters partnership location and serves as the main coordinator for all the work done there as part of the Urban Waters program. Urban Waters nonpartnership location means an urban or city site and the surrounding watershed or body of water that gets federal money for Urban Waters program activities but is not officially designated as an Urban Waters partnership location and does not have an active partnership with an Urban Waters ambassador or a workplan. Urban Waters partnership location means an urban or city site and the surrounding watershed or body of water that has been officially designated by the Administrator working with other member agencies as a partnership location under the Urban Waters program and has an active partnership with an Urban Waters ambassador. Urban Waters partnership location workplan means the plan that lists projects and actions that are coordinated across an Urban Waters partnership location. Urban Waters program means the program created under Section 3 part a of this law.

sections.title: Section 3  Urban Waters Federal Partnership Program
sections.text: Part a creates a program called the Urban Waters Federal Partnership Program. This program is run by a partnership of all the member agencies. The program has two main goals. First, it supports and carries out the goals of the Urban Waters program using the independent powers and funding that each member agency already has. Second, it advances the program's purpose in designated Urban Waters partnership locations and other urban and suburban communities across the United States. Part b explains that the purpose of the Urban Waters program is to reconnect urban communities with nearby waterways by improving coordination among federal agencies. The program especially focuses on urban communities that are overburdened or economically distressed. Part c explains the program requirements. The Administrator must work with the Secretaries and other member agency heads to maintain the Urban Waters program as long as Congress provides funding. The Administrator must create a steering committee for the Urban Waters program. The Administrator serves as the chairperson and the Secretaries serve as vice-chairpersons. Other members include senior officials from member agencies that the Administrator chooses. The steering committee provides general guidance to member agencies about the Urban Waters program. This includes identifying annual priority issues for special emphasis within Urban Waters partnership locations and identifying funding opportunities that are shared with all Urban Waters partnership locations. Member agencies can provide money to the steering committee and transfer funds as needed to support the steering committee's activities, even though other laws might normally prevent this. The Administrator and Secretaries must maintain an active partnership program at each Urban Waters partnership location, including those that already exist when this law is passed. They do this by providing technical assistance for projects, funding for projects, funding for an Urban Waters ambassador, and coordination support with other member agencies. The Administrator and Secretaries can create new Urban Waters partnership locations after talking with other member agency heads. A community with an Urban Waters nonpartnership location can ask to have their location designated as an Urban Waters partnership location if they want to. An eligible entity means a state, territory or possession of the United States, the District of Columbia, an Indian Tribe, a unit of local government, a public or private college or university, a public or private nonprofit organization, an intertribal consortium, an interstate agency, or any other entity the Administrator thinks is appropriate. Member agencies can encourage, cooperate with, and provide technical services and financial help to Urban Waters ambassadors and eligible entities. Urban Waters ambassadors can bring together federal and non-federal partners for the location, develop and carry out a workplan, use available federal and non-federal resources for projects, and share information and best practices with the Urban Waters Learning Network. Eligible entities can carry out projects at Urban Waters partnership locations that improve habitat or water quality, increase river recreation, enhance community resiliency, install infrastructure, strengthen community engagement with and education about water resources, or support planning, coordination, and execution of projects in the workplan. They can also do planning, research, experiments, demonstrations, surveys, studies, monitoring, training, and outreach to advance the program's purpose at both partnership and nonpartnership locations. Member agencies can transfer funds to or make agreements with other member agencies as needed to carry out the Urban Waters program. The Administrator and Secretaries must maintain an Urban Waters Learning Network to share information, resources, and tools between Urban Waters partnership locations and with other interested communities. The network also carries out community-based capacity building that advances the program's goals. Progress in addressing the goals of each Urban Waters partnership location workplan must be shared with the Urban Waters program at regular intervals that the Administrator and Secretaries decide. Part d requires the Administrator and Secretaries to submit an annual report to the appropriate committees of Congress describing progress in carrying out the Urban Waters program. The report must include a description of how funds were used, progress made in carrying out workplans, and any additional information the Administrator and Secretaries think is appropriate. Part e authorizes Congress to provide ten million dollars to the Administrator for each fiscal year from 2026 through 2030 to carry out the Urban Waters program. Money provided to the Administrator under this section can be used together with money from other member agencies and non-federal entities that participate in the Urban Waters program, even if other laws might normally prevent this.
